title: Different Proxies, Different Domains: Pollen and Charcoal Exceedance Reveal Complementary Human Impacts at 111 Same-Core Sites
abstract: Extending the identifiability framework from intra-proxy to inter-proxy comparison, we analyse 111 same-core pollen-charcoal pairs from Neotoma. Charcoal exceedance at 22 sites (19.8%) where pollen detects no agricultural signal demonstrates complementary detection domains. Fisher's exact p=0.002, OR=3.51. Union detection rate (54.1%) exceeds either proxy alone. Introduces Type D (fire regime) as fourth transformation domain.
novelty_statement: First systematic same-core comparison of pollen and charcoal exceedance at 111 sites. 20% of human impact signals are detectable only by charcoal, invisible to pollen. Extends the identifiability framework to inter-proxy complementarity.
